name: Swift compiler & CLI
description: The Swift compiler and package manager CLI — build, run, test, and publish Swift packages
when_to_use: Every Swift project outside of Xcode; also inside Xcode projects via `swift package`

install.command: Install Xcode or Swift toolchain from swift.org

name: SwiftLint
description: A tool to enforce Swift style and conventions — based on the GitHub Swift Style Guide with 200+ rules
when_to_use: Any Swift project that wants automated style checking in editor or CI

name: SwiftFormat
description: Rewrites Swift source files according to a configurable set of rules — complements SwiftLint
when_to_use: Swift projects that want opinionated automatic formatting

name: xcodebuild
description: Xcode's command-line build and test driver for Swift and Objective-C projects
when_to_use: When building iOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS apps from CI or scripts

install.command: Install Xcode from the Mac App Store

name: fastlane
description: Automation toolchain for iOS/Android app release workflows — beta distribution, app store deployment, screenshot automation
when_to_use: iOS/Android mobile CI; anything involving App Store Connect or Google Play

name: Swift Testing
description: Apple's modern testing framework for Swift — uses @Test and @Suite macros with expressive #expect assertions, replacing XCTest for new projects
when_to_use: For Swift 5.9+ projects that want modern, expressive test syntax with parameterized tests and built-in concurrency support

name: xcresultparser
description: Parses Xcode .xcresult bundles into human-readable formats — extracts test results, coverage data, and diagnostics for CI reporting
when_to_use: In CI pipelines to convert Xcode test result bundles into JUnit XML, Cobertura coverage, or markdown reports

name: Periphery
description: Detects unused code in Swift projects — finds unused classes, functions, protocols, properties, and enum cases across the entire project
when_to_use: During code cleanup to identify dead code that can be safely removed from Swift projects

name: Swift Argument Parser
description: Apple's library for parsing command-line arguments in Swift — type-safe, declarative API with automatic help generation and shell completions
when_to_use: When building Swift CLI tools that need argument parsing with validation, subcommands, and generated help text

name: SourceKit-LSP
description: Apple's official Language Server Protocol implementation for Swift and C-family languages — provides code completion, jump-to-definition, and diagnostics
when_to_use: Always active for Swift projects in LSP-compatible editors — provides IDE intelligence without Xcode

name: Swift-DocC
description: Apple's documentation compiler for Swift — generates rich documentation from doc comments, tutorials, and articles with a built-in web renderer
when_to_use: To generate and preview API documentation for Swift packages and frameworks

name: Vapor
description: Server-side Swift web framework — provides routing, middleware, ORM (Fluent), templating (Leaf), and WebSocket support for building HTTP APIs and web apps
when_to_use: For building server-side Swift web applications and REST APIs

name: Tuist
description: Xcode project generator and build system for Swift — define projects in Swift DSL, generate .xcodeproj files, manage dependencies, and optimize build times
when_to_use: For large iOS/macOS projects that need reproducible Xcode project generation and modular architecture

name: CocoaPods
description: Dependency manager for iOS/macOS projects — resolves and installs libraries from Podfile using a centralized spec repository
when_to_use: For iOS/macOS projects that use CocoaPods for dependency management (legacy but still widely used)

name: Carthage
description: Decentralized dependency manager for iOS/macOS — builds frameworks from source without modifying your Xcode project, giving you full control
when_to_use: For iOS/macOS projects that prefer a lightweight, non-intrusive dependency manager over CocoaPods or SPM
